title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Julian Harmon and Roundy share a conversation where Roundy recounts how a local resident, Mr. Bartlett, has begun attending church specifically to hear Julian preach, noting that the congregation respects Julian's Oxford education. As Roundy departs, she sings a song about murder being on the scene.

In a courtyard, Miss Blacklock, the mistress of the house, eats breakfast with Julia Simmons, Patrick Simmons, and her friend Miss Dora Bunner. The group is interrupted when Miss Bunner discovers an unusual notice in the newspaper. Miss Blacklock reads the notice aloud: "A murder will take place at Little Park on Friday, October 29th at 6:30 p.m." Miss Blacklock initially suspects Patrick of playing a prank, but he denies it. While Miss Bunner is distressed by the perceived cruelty of the notice, Miss Blacklock dismisses it as a tasteless joke, though she expresses interest in finding the culprit. Patrick grimly jokes that the "murder" might refer to the "Death of Delight," a special cake made by Micky.

Later, Miss Blacklock reflects on her relationship with Miss Bunner. The two were school friends, but Miss Bunner’s life became difficult, leaving her in financial distress and poor health. Six months prior, Miss Blacklock responded to a sorrowful letter from Miss Bunner by bringing her to live at the house to help manage the household. Despite Miss Bunner's loyalty, she is unreliable, muddled, and often irritates Miss Blacklock with her incompetence. As Miss Bunner expresses anxiety over the "cruel" advertisement, Miss Blacklock views her as a lovable but over-sensitive and intuitive old fool.
